steps.title: Locate the verb-object boundary
steps.content: The blank follows the verb compiled and precedes the direct object “climate data from several sites in Puerto Rico’s Luquillo Mountains.” There is nothing between them — no subordinate clause, no list, no inserted element.

steps.title: Apply the verb-object punctuation rule
steps.content:
Standard English does not allow punctuation between a verb and its direct object.
  • Choice A — 'compiled,': a comma between the verb and its object violates this rule. ✗
  • Choice B — 'compiled': no punctuation; compiled connects directly to climate data, conforming to Standard English. ✓
  • Choice C — 'compiled:': a colon between the verb and its object violates this rule. ✗
  • Choice D — 'compiled.': a period ends the sentence before the object is stated, creating an incomplete sentence. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
